title: Peppermint and jojoba scalp oil
subtitle: A cooling scalp treatment oil with peppermint and rosemary essential oils in a jojoba base
excerpt: Jojoba oil has a wax ester structure similar to the scalp's natural sebum, which makes it a good base for a scalp treatment: it spreads evenly, absorbs without a heavy residue, and carries essential oils efficiently. Peppermint gives a cooling, tingling sensation on application; rosemary essential oil is added alongside it. Yield is 50 ml in a dropper bottle.
sourceNotes: Jojoba (Simmondsia chinensis) as a scalp oil base: technically a liquid wax, not an oil; closely mimics sebum structure. Shelf life 2 years or more without refrigeration. Peppermint EO at 3% in a leave-on scalp product: within the typical usage range for cosmetics (IFRA guidance does not restrict peppermint in rinse-off at this rate, but leave-on application requires care; 3% is the upper limit for most scalp formulations). Rosemary EO (Rosmarinus officinalis): not to be used by people with epilepsy or during pregnancy; avoid on infants. Do not use on broken skin.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale by multiplying all weights. Use one dropper bottle per 50 ml batch.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Label with the date made.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 to 24 months in a cool, dark place. Jojoba is one of the most stable carrier oils. Discard if the oil develops a rancid or off smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: A plant-derived oil used to dilute essential oils before applying to skin. Essential oils are too concentrated to use undiluted; a carrier oil disperses them safely and provides its own nourishing or conditioning properties. Jojoba, sweet almond, rosehip, and fractionated coconut oil are all common carriers.

content.text: Jojoba is technically a liquid wax rather than an oil, which gives it an unusually long shelf life and a structure closely related to the scalp's own sebum. As a

for a scalp treatment, it spreads evenly and absorbs without the heaviness of denser oils. Peppermint essential oil gives a pronounced cooling, tingling sensation on the scalp; rosemary essential oil is added for its own properties. Both are diluted to a combined 6% of the jojoba weight, which is within the safe range for a leave-on scalp product. Do not use rosemary essential oil during pregnancy, on infants, or if epilepsy is a concern. Avoid contact with eyes.

items.prepNote: Golden or clear jojoba; both work equally well here
3% of jojoba weight; cooling, tingling on application
3% of jojoba weight; avoid in pregnancy, epilepsy, or on infants

content.text: Weigh the jojoba oil into a small bowl or directly into the dropper bottle using a funnel. Add the peppermint and rosemary essential oils and stir briefly to combine. If blending in a bowl, use the funnel to decant into the dropper bottle. Seal and label with the date.

Apply 8 to 10 drops to the scalp, parting the hair in sections. Massage in with fingertips for 2 to 3 minutes. Leave for 30 minutes or overnight, then wash out with shampoo. The peppermint gives a noticeable cooling sensation on contact; this is normal and not an irritant response. Avoid contact with eyes. If any irritation develops on the scalp, rinse off and do not use again.

Store in the dropper bottle with the lid sealed, away from direct sunlight and heat. Jojoba has a very long shelf life and the blend will keep for 12 to 24 months. Discard if the oil smells rancid or off.
